text:
The athletics is in full swing at theParis2024Olympics, with the biggest stars in track and fieldeyeing glory at the Stade de France.

The sport is amongst the most popular of any Games, and those tuning in to watch from the comfort of their own sofas may have noticed a green card being issued to athletes at the start of some races.

A green card is one of several cards which can be given to athletes during competition, but what are the meanings behind them?

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Here is everything you need to know.

If athletes are recalled following a faulty start but no competitor was at fault, such as when there is an issue with the machine used to catch false starts, then the whole field will be given a green card.

The athletes are sent back to the starting blocks and the race is restarted.

Athletes are shown a yellow card if they are adjudged to have caused the start of a race to be aborted.

The issuing of the card is used to signify that an athlete has been handed a personal warning for improper conduct.

Examples of this include sitting up in the blocks, holding their hand up, deliberately delaying the start or making a noise.

Should the athlete commit another offence, they will be shown a red card and subsequently disqualified.

The diagonal red and black card is given to athletes when a false start has been made, disqualifying them from the race.

A false start is defined when an athlete moves before, or within 0.1 seconds, of the starting gun.

Previously, a false start would result in a warning for the entire field, with any resultant false start seeing the athlete at fault disqualified.

The rules have now changed, however, with one false start resulting in instant disqualification.

An athlete can protest against the disqualification or run under protest, with the disqualification reviewed at the end of the race.

title: What does a green card mean in athletics at the Olympics? | Metro News
description: Find out what a green card means in athletics, with several already shown to athletes at the Paris 2024 Olympics at the Stade de France.
